final_answer: COVID-19 can lead to serious complications and death, unlike the common cold or flu, which are generally less severe. COVID-19 symptoms can include loss of taste or smell, which is not typical for the common cold or flu. The virus causing COVID-19, SARS-CoV-2, is genetically distinct from the viruses that cause the common cold and flu. COVID-19 spreads primarily through respiratory droplets, similar to the flu, but can also spread from asymptomatic individuals. COVID-19 can cause severe illness in people with pre-existing conditions, while colds and flu generally have milder outcomes.
